Interview Guide


Intro [5 min]
* My name is Jin, and I’m a product researcher . 
* Thank you for participating in the early access program and providing feedback on our new  feature called the AI summary. This feature can automate the analysis of your survey data.  
* This feature is still a work in progress. Your feedback can help us meaningfully improve it. Today, I want to understand specifically about your perceptions of the report generated by the feature. 
* This session will be 60 minutes. You see a notetaking app running that allows a few of my colleagues to observe our session to help me take notes so I can focus 100% on our conversation
* Is it ok if I record this session for internal use? I would like to ask you to log into your SVMK and share your screen and how you might use the feature. I hope you feel comfortable to do so. 


Part 1: Background [10 min]
* To get started: Tell us what you do, and how you work with SurveyMonkey?
* Walk me through what your typical survey process looks like, starting from survey programming to data analysis.  
* Have you tried the feature before our session? 
   * If yes: 
      1. Tell me about the recent survey where you’ve used the feature
      2. What was the goal of the survey? Are the results analyzed? 
   * If no:  
      1. Why don’t we use this time to try the feature on one of your surveys together? Let’s log into SVMK and pull up a survey where you’d like to use the feature. I’ll give you a few minutes. Feel free to let me know if you want me to walk through anything with you.
      2. Tell me about the survey; What was the goal of the survey? Are the results analyzed? 
* Let’s take a look at the generated report together. I’ll give you a moment to pull it up, and feel free to screen-share when you’re ready.


Part 2: Feature usefulness, accuracy, and trust [30 min]
*  Let me give you a few moments for you to review this section-by-section.
* Imagine that I am your colleague, and this is my first time seeing the report. Can you walk me briefly through it section-by-section?
* Tell me about your general impression of the report. 
* Overall, how accurate did the report seem?  
   * What, if anything, stood out to you as accurate or inaccurate?
   * How did you try to assess whether it was accurate?
* How much do you trust the insights that were generated? Why is that?
   * Would you normally take any steps to validate reports like this? What would that look like for you?
* Overall, how useful did you find the report? 
   * What sections were useful? 
   * What sections were not useful? 
   * Is there an example of an insight that felt difficult to act on? What was missing to make it actionable?   
   * Is there an example of an insight that felt like things you already knew? What, if anything, value did they add?
* Looking at this report overall: 
   * Is there an example of an insight that you think is missing? Something that you would want to see in your own report?  
   * Is there an example of an insight that you want to remove? That you’d never include in your own report? 
Part 4: [Workflow integration] [20 min]
* [if meaningful actual behaviors] Tell me about the last time you used this feature before our session. Think of that time, and tell me as much detail as you can remember.
   * What happened after viewing this report? Edit/share/export
   * How, if at all, did that summary affect those actions?
   * Tell me about that—what specific piece of info was it, and what did you do with it?
   * You mentioned the summary was interesting—did it lead you to do anything differently than you originally intended?
* [if no meaningful actual behaviors] Earlier you described how you typically work with surveys on this platform: [describe]. How do you think this feature fits into that process?
   * Where would it fit? What would it replace, if anything?
   * What doesn’t work for you about it? What would need to change for it to fit?
   * How do these two workflows compare in terms of ease of completing your job?
   * What makes one feel easier or harder, if at all?
   * What are the first three actions you would take after viewing the report in that process?
   * How would you describe the feature? Is this a person whom you would never meet, a person whom you only meet once, or a person whom you would continuously meet? Why is that?


Closing [X min]
* How likely are you to recommend this feature to your colleagues? Why is that?
If time 
Part 3: [Engagement] [X min]
* Is being able to customize this AI summary important to you? Why is that?
   * How would you customize the report?  
   * Why are those customization options important?
